metadata.title: Checkbox
metadata.description: The checkbox component provides a clear way for users to make selections, such as agreeing to terms, enabling settings, or choosing multiple items from a list. Use checkbox to create binary on/off controls or multi-select interfaces where users can select any combination of options.

properties.description: Whether the checkbox displays in an indeterminate state (neither checked nor unchecked), typically used to indicate partial selection in hierarchical lists. This visual state takes priority over the checked prop in appearance only. The form submission value is still determined by the checked prop. If indeterminate has not been explicitly set and hasn't been modified by user interaction, it returns the value of defaultIndeterminate.

The initial indeterminate state for uncontrolled components. Use this when you want the checkbox to start in an indeterminate state but don't need to control it afterward. This value applies until indeterminate is explicitly set or the user changes the checkbox state by clicking.
Whether the control is currently checked. Use this for controlled components where you manage the checked state.
The value used in form data when the checkbox is checked.
The initial checked state for uncontrolled components. Use this when you want the control to start checked but don't need to control its state afterward.

A label that describes the purpose or content of the component for assistive technologies like screen readers. Use this to provide additional context when the visible content alone doesn't clearly convey the component's purpose.

Supplementary text displayed below the checkbox to provide additional context, instructions, or help. Use this to explain what checking the box means or provide guidance to users. This text is announced to screen readers.
An error message displayed below the checkbox to indicate validation problems. When set, the checkbox is styled with error indicators and the message is announced to screen readers.
The text label displayed next to the checkbox that describes what the checkbox controls. Clicking the label will also toggle the checkbox state.

Whether the field needs a value. This requirement adds semantic value to the field, but it will not cause an error to appear automatically. If you want to present an error when this field is empty, you can do so with the error property.
Whether the field is disabled, preventing any user interaction.
A unique identifier for the element. Use this to reference the element in JavaScript, link labels to form controls, or target specific elements for styling or scripting.
The name attribute for the field, used to identify the field's value when the form is submitted. Must be unique within the nearest containing form.

examples.name: Main example
examples.html_code:
<s-checkbox
  label="Require a confirmation step"
  details="Ensure all criteria are met before proceeding"
></s-checkbox>
examples.erb_code: <%= form.check_box :require_a_confirmation_step, label: "Require a confirmation step", details: "Ensure all criteria are met before proceeding" %>

examples.name: Select an option
examples.description: Let users toggle a single option on or off. This example displays a checkbox with a label and helper text providing additional context.
examples.html_code:
<s-checkbox
  label="Require a confirmation step"
  details="Ensure all criteria are met before proceeding"
></s-checkbox>
examples.erb_code: <%= form.check_box :require_a_confirmation_step, label: "Require a confirmation step", details: "Ensure all criteria are met before proceeding" %>

examples.name: Show an indeterminate state
examples.description: Indicate partial selection in bulk actions. This example displays a "select all" checkbox in an indeterminate state when some items are checked.
examples.html_code:
<s-stack gap="small">
  <s-checkbox
    label="Select all items"
    indeterminate
  ></s-checkbox>
  <s-divider></s-divider>
  <s-checkbox label="Item 1" checked></s-checkbox>
  <s-checkbox label="Item 2"></s-checkbox>
  <s-checkbox label="Item 3"></s-checkbox>
</s-stack>
examples.erb_code:
<s-stack gap="small">
  <%= form.check_box :select_all_items, label: "Select all items", indeterminate: true %>
  <s-divider></s-divider>
  <%= form.check_box :item_1, label: "Item 1", checked: true %>
  <%= form.check_box :item_2, label: "Item 2" %>
  <%= form.check_box :item_3, label: "Item 3" %>
</s-stack>

examples.name: Show a validation error
examples.description: Communicate when a required selection is missing. This example displays an error message when the terms checkbox isn't checked.
examples.html_code:
<s-checkbox
  label="I agree to the terms"
  error="You must accept the terms to continue"
></s-checkbox>
examples.erb_code: <%= form.check_box :i_agree_to_the_terms, label: "I agree to the terms", error: "You must accept the terms to continue" %>

examples.name: Show a disabled checkbox
examples.description: Indicate when an option isn't available. This example presents a disabled checkbox with helper text explaining how to enable it.
examples.html_code:
<s-checkbox
  label="Advanced settings"
  disabled
  details="Contact your administrator to enable advanced settings"
></s-checkbox>
examples.erb_code: <%= form.check_box :advanced_settings, label: "Advanced settings", disabled: true, details: "Contact your administrator to enable advanced settings" %>

examples.name: Group multiple checkboxes
examples.description: Organize related options together. This example groups multiple checkboxes in a settings panel with individual helper text.
examples.html_code:
<s-stack gap="base">
  <s-checkbox label="Show only published products" checked></s-checkbox>
  <s-checkbox
    label="Enable inventory tracking"
    details="Track inventory levels and receive low stock alerts"
    checked
  ></s-checkbox>
  <s-checkbox
    label="View customer data"
    details="Allow staff to access customer contact information and order history"
  ></s-checkbox>
</s-stack>
examples.erb_code:
<s-stack gap="base">
  <%= form.check_box :show_only_published_products, label: "Show only published products", checked: true %>
  <%= form.check_box :enable_inventory_tracking, label: "Enable inventory tracking", details: "Track inventory levels and receive low stock alerts", checked: true %>
  <%= form.check_box :view_customer_data, label: "View customer data", details: "Allow staff to access customer contact information and order history" %>
</s-stack>

examples.name: Validate in real time
examples.description: Provide immediate feedback on required selections. This example demonstrates validation with an error message when the checkbox is unchecked.
examples.html_code:
<s-section>
  <s-stack gap="base" justifyContent="start">
    <s-text-field label="Name"></s-text-field>
    <s-checkbox
      label="I agree to the terms"
      error="You must accept the terms to continue"
    ></s-checkbox>
  </s-stack>
</s-section>
examples.erb_code:
<s-section>
  <s-stack gap="base" justifycontent="start">
    <%= form.text_field :name, label: "Name" %>
    <%= form.check_box :i_agree_to_the_terms, label: "I agree to the terms", error: "You must accept the terms to continue" %>
  </s-stack>
</s-section>
